review_comment:
I don't think there's anything that requires the local directory to be a repository, so this doesn't seem quite right.

That said I understand that the short and use indicate that it's a repository, so let's just leave it alone.

I had this thought too and here is how I rationalized it to myself:

I saw us using `repository` to describe the directory as a suggestion that we want extension authors to consider their extensions as repositories, even if we don't strictly require it. We especially want them to consider them as _GitHub_ repositories, except in this case it is cloned locally.

Maybe there's holes on my logic 🤪 , but that was my thoughts and why I didn't change it.

I'll mark this thread as resolved, but feel free to re-open it if you think we have something to do here 🙏
Do you think it's worth doc-ing how this is different on windows?

@jtmcg and I [also wondered about that](https://github.com/cli/cli/pull/9933#discussion_r1847049096) but landed on it being an implementation detail that may confuse the docs if we try to document it. 

What do you think?
@williammartin and I talked about this and decided to add a hint to the help text without fully documenting the differences.
